description: This classic chocolate chip cookie recipe is easy to make and yields perfectly soft and chewy cookies every time. With a perfect balance of sweetness and richness, these cookies are ideal for any occasion, from a casual snack to a special treat.
ingredients: [{"name": "Butter", "unit": "g", "amount": "115"}, {"name": "White Sugar", "unit": "g", "amount": "100"}, {"name": "Brown Sugar", "unit": "g", "amount": "150"}, {"name": "Large Egg", "unit": null, "amount": "1"}, {"name": "Vanilla Extract", "unit": "tsp", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "All-Purpose Flour", "unit": "g", "amount": "200"}, {"name": "Baking Soda", "unit": "tsp", "amount": "0.5"}, {"name": "Salt", "unit": "tsp", "amount": "0.5"}, {"name": "Chocolate Chips", "unit": "g", "amount": "200"}]
instructions: Preheat your oven to 190°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. In a bowl, combine the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside. Cream together softened butter, white sugar, and brown sugar until fluffy. Add the egg and vanilla extract, beating until well combined. Gradually mix in the dry ingredients until just combined. Fold in the chocolate chips evenly. Scoop tablespoon-sized portions of dough and roll them into balls, placing them on the prepared baking sheet. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until the edges are golden, but the centers are still soft. Let the cookies cool on the sheet for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
notes: Do not overmix the dough to avoid flat cookies. If you prefer chewier cookies, remove them from the oven slightly earlier. For a richer flavor, use dark chocolate chips or a mix of milk and dark chocolate.
